mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off positioning and earnings downgrade expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of housing starts and revolving credit balances captures aggregate consumer demand strength. When both series expand simultaneously, corporate revenue growth accelerates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals supply-side capacity utilization. In a late-cycle expansion regime, rising INDPRO values indicate bottleneck pressures that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during late-cycle periods, contradicting the margin compression thesis.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth provides a continuous trend signal for corporate earnings momentum. Higher GDPC1 readings directly correlate with improved cash flow projections, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between quarterly GDPC1 revisions and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.05 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital structurally elevates, suppressing equity valuations and delaying capital expenditure cycles. This threshold breach predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand or remain stable despite sustained FEDFUNDS readings above 4.5%, invalidating the discount rate transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between nonfarm payrolls growth and the unemployment rate reveals labor market friction. When PAYEMS rises but UNRATE simultaneously increases, wage-push inflation expectations emerge, forcing rate hike fears and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistically significant predictive power for 20-day forward returns after controlling for baseline market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, housing starts act as a leading indicator for consumer confidence and durable goods spending. Rising HOUST values trigger positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: HOUST expansions fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 40% of post-recession episodes, breaking the leading indicator relationship.
